patterns.description: Now-playing row pinned at top of the ordered queue list. DJ advances pointer with 'Next' button. MVP: no real iframe playback, only pointer state.
Drag handles, approve/reject, reorder, delete visible only when effectiveRole in ('owner','editor'). Non-DJ students see submit button + cancel-own-pending only.
queueStatus shown as inline pill. pending=gray, approved=green, played=muted with timestamp, rejected=red or hidden (by filter).
Avatar + name of the student who submitted, reusing Card.authors[]. Teacher-submitted items show teacher avatar.
YouTube thumbnail 120x68 (16:9) as leftmost element. Title truncated to 2 lines, channel name secondary.
Drag-and-drop reorder with visible handle. Keyboard fallback out of scope for MVP.
Reorder/approve/delete applies local state immediately, reconciles with server. Rollback on HTTP failure. Pending IDs exempted from SSE snapshot merge to avoid stomping.

rejected_patterns.reason: Requires WebRTC/WS for frame-accurate sync; out of scope. MVP uses pointer-only 'now playing' semantics.
Overkill for classroom scenario; one or few DJs cover the need. Data model supports future extension.
